nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for the Camping Dashboard Next.js application, describing setup instructions and the tech stack used.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package manifest defining dependencies (Next.js 16, React 19, Supabase, Leaflet, MapTiler, TailwindCSS v4) and build scripts for the camping dashboard application.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode with the @/ path alias mapping to ./src for clean imports throughout the project.

nodes.name: vercel.json
nodes.summary: Vercel deployment configuration defining cron job schedules for automated weather and alert data refresh endpoints.

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration extending Next.js core web vitals and TypeScript rules for code quality enforcement.

nodes.name: next.config.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js configuration file enabling TypeScript-based config with minimal overrides for the App Router setup.

nodes.name: postcss.config.mjs
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the TailwindCSS v4 PostCSS plugin for CSS processing.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route handler that scrapes Algonquin Park alerts from the provincial parks website and syncs them to the Supabase alerts table, deactivating stale entries.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js API route that fetches current weather and 5-day forecast data from Open-Meteo API for the Maple Lake coordinates, then upserts records into Supabase weather tables.

nodes.name: route.ts
nodes.summary: Supabase OAuth callback handler that exchanges the auth code for a session and redirects the user to the dashboard.

nodes.name: globals.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS design system defining the Day/Night theme token variables (colors, fonts), topographic background layers, custom scrollbar styles, and the CRUD slide-out sheet form component styles.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (variables) for dual-theme switching and TailwindCSS v4 @theme blocks for token registration.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Root Next.js App Router layout that imports Google Fonts (Inter and JetBrains Mono), sets HTML metadata, and wraps the application in the global CSS context.

nodes.name: page.module.css
nodes.summary: CSS module providing dashboard grid layout styles, background topography overlay, and card positioning utilities for the main page.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Main dashboard page orchestrating all 13 card components. Manages Supabase data fetching, optimistic state for all user-authored entities (gear, meals, timeline, crew, alerts, offline status, park intel), auth-gated CRUD mutations, and the live countdown timer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a DashboardLoader/DashboardContent split to handle async Supabase data loading with a cinematic spinner before hydration.

nodes.name: DashboardLoader
nodes.summary: React component that fetches all dashboard data from Supabase on mount, shows a compass-themed loading animation, and renders DashboardContent once data is ready.

nodes.name: DashboardContent
nodes.summary: Core dashboard component holding all state slices and CRUD mutation handlers. Composes the full 12-column grid layout of all feature cards, with auth-gated edit controls and the readiness score engine.

nodes.name: AlertFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for creating manual alerts with title, body, severity level, and source fields.

nodes.name: AlertsCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card displaying active trip alerts with severity color-coding (info/warning/critical), delete controls for manual entries, and a button to add new manual alerts.

nodes.name: AstroCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card showing astronomical data: golden hour window, moon phase and illumination percentage, Milky Way visibility, and computed sky quality rating.

nodes.name: CountdownCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card rendering a live countdown clock to trip departure, displaying days, hours, minutes, and seconds with a mission-style display.

nodes.name: CrewFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for adding or editing crew member records including name, role, canoe assignment, load item, and weight.

nodes.name: CrewRosterCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card displaying the expedition crew roster grouped by canoe, showing each member's role, gear load, and weight with edit/delete controls.

nodes.name: ForecastCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card showing a 5-day weather forecast as a horizontal row of mini forecast tiles with high/low temps, rain chance, and condition icons.

nodes.name: GearChecklistCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card displaying the gear checklist grouped by category, with pack/unpack toggles, priority color indicators, and CRUD controls for adding/editing/deleting gear items.

nodes.name: GearFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for creating or editing gear items with name, category, owner, priority, weight, and packed status fields.

nodes.name: HeroHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard hero header displaying the trip identity (park, lake, site), current weather summary, overall readiness score badge, live countdown, and buttons for Mission Brief and auth login.

nodes.name: MapRouteCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Client-side wrapper that dynamically loads the Leaflet map component with SSR disabled to avoid hydration errors.

nodes.name: MapRouteCardInner.tsx
nodes.summary: Leaflet-based interactive map showing the canoe route from launch point to campsite with markers and a polyline, using MapTiler vector tiles as the base layer.

nodes.name: MealFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for planning meals with day number, meal type, title, prep type, calories, and assignment fields.

nodes.name: MealPlannerCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card organizing meals by day and type (breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack), showing prep type icons, calorie counts, and CRUD controls.

nodes.name: OfflineVaultCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card showing offline readiness checklist (maps cached, permit saved, route downloaded, satellite device, emergency contact) with toggle controls and a link to the Project Intel modal.

nodes.name: ParkIntelCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card displaying park intelligence: fire restrictions, wildlife notes, water quality, firewood availability percentage, and ranger station info with inline editing support.

nodes.name: ParkIntelFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for editing park intelligence fields including fire restriction status, wildlife notes, water notes, firewood percentage, and custom notes.

nodes.name: ReadinessScoreCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card visualizing the weighted expedition readiness score (0â€“100) with five component bars: Gear (35%), Offline Safety (20%), Meals (20%), Weather (15%), and Timeline (10%), plus a readiness label.

nodes.name: ScoreBar
nodes.summary: Renders a labeled progress bar for a single readiness component with color-coded fill based on score threshold.

nodes.name: TimelineCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card showing the trip timeline grouped by day, with events sorted by time, phase color-coding (Transit/Setup/Sustain/Leisure), and CRUD controls for adding/editing/deleting events.

nodes.name: TimelineFormSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-out CRUD form for creating or editing timeline events with day number, time, title, details, phase, and sort order fields.

nodes.name: WeatherCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Dashboard card displaying current weather conditions: temperature, wind speed, humidity, rain chance, visibility, sunrise/sunset times, and the moon phase from astro data.

nodes.name: ChecklistItem.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable checklist row component with a toggle checkbox, item label, optional priority badge, and edit/delete action buttons shown on hover.

nodes.name: CrudSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared slide-out panel primitive providing the overlay, panel container, header with close button, and scrollable body for all CRUD form sheets.

nodes.name: GlassCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight glassmorphism card wrapper component applying background blur, border, and shadow styles to any child content.

nodes.name: IconLabel.tsx
nodes.summary: Simple UI primitive pairing a Lucide icon with a text label in a flex row for consistent metric display.

nodes.name: MetricRow.tsx
nodes.summary: UI primitive rendering a label-value pair in a horizontal row with muted label and prominent value styling.

nodes.name: MiniForecastDay.tsx
nodes.summary: Compact forecast tile showing a single day's weather: date, condition icon, high/low temps, and rain probability.

nodes.name: MissionBriefModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen modal providing a mission briefing overlay with trip objectives, key logistics, emergency protocols, and expedition rules in a structured tabbed layout.

nodes.name: Primitives.tsx
nodes.summary: Collection of three shared UI primitives: Badge (colored status badge), Divider (horizontal separator), and Tooltip (hover text display).

nodes.name: ProgressBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated progress bar component with configurable fill color, percentage value display, and smooth CSS transition.

nodes.name: ProjectIntelModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-screen modal documenting the technical architecture of the Camping Dashboard project itself â€” stack, data flow, schema, and deployment details â€” acting as an in-app living documentation panel.

nodes.name: SectionHeader.tsx
nodes.summary: Card section header primitive with a title, optional icon, and an optional action button slot for CRUD triggers.

nodes.name: StatusPill.tsx
nodes.summary: Small colored pill badge for displaying status labels (e.g. severity levels, priority states) with variant-based color mapping.

nodes.name: TopoBackground.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG-based topographic map background component generating procedural contour lines using fractal noise, creating the wilderness mission-control aesthetic behind the dashboard.

nodes.name: authContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context providing auth state and the isAuthorized flag to control which components can perform write operations. Wraps Supabase session management and exposes a login trigger.

nodes.name: AuthProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider that subscribes to Supabase auth state changes, exposes isAuthorized and a signIn handler, and wraps child components.

nodes.name: fetchDashboard.ts
nodes.summary: Batch-fetches all 12 Supabase tables in parallel (trips, weather, forecast, gear, timeline, meals, crew, park intel, offline status, astro data, alerts, settings) and returns a unified DashboardData object.

nodes.name: fetchDashboardData
nodes.summary: Dispatches 12 parallel Supabase queries using Promise.all and assembles the full DashboardData payload, throwing on trip fetch failure.

nodes.name: helpers.ts
nodes.summary: Pure derived-logic library containing all dashboard calculations: countdown timer, readiness scoring engine (gear/meals/offline/timeline/weather), theme mode detection, astro quality helpers, date formatters, and generic utilities.

nodes.name: calculateOverallReadiness
nodes.summary: Computes the weighted overall readiness score from five component scores (gear 35%, meals 20%, weather 15%, offline 20%, timeline 10%) and returns a ReadinessScore object with label.

nodes.name: getTripCountdown
nodes.summary: Calculates days/hours/minutes/seconds remaining until trip start date, parsing at local noon to avoid UTC-midnight timezone issues.

nodes.name: mutations.ts
nodes.summary: Supabase CRUD mutation functions for all user-authored entities: gear items, meals, timeline events, crew members, alerts, offline status, and park intel. All functions are strongly typed and return Supabase response objects.

nodes.name: supabase.ts
nodes.summary: Browser-side Supabase client singleton created with public ANON key for use in client components and auth flows.

nodes.name: supabaseAdmin.ts
nodes.summary: Server-side Supabase admin client using the SERVICE_ROLE key, used exclusively in API route handlers for privileged database operations like weather and alert syncing.

nodes.name: weatherMapper.ts
nodes.summary: Maps raw Open-Meteo API response data to the Supabase weather_current and weather_forecast schema formats, including WMO weather code to condition label/icon translation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses WMO (World Meteorological Organization) weather interpretation codes to produce human-readable condition labels and emoji icons.

nodes.name: mapCurrentWeather
nodes.summary: Transforms the Open-Meteo current_weather object into the weather_current Supabase schema, including derived fields like rain_chance, visibility, and formatted times.

nodes.name: mapForecast
nodes.summary: Transforms Open-Meteo daily forecast arrays into an array of weather_forecast rows for Supabase, one record per forecast date.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Central TypeScript type definitions for the entire dashboard: Trip, WeatherCurrent, WeatherForecast, GearItem, Meal, TimelineEvent, CrewMember, ParkIntel, OfflineStatus, AstroData, Alert, Settings, DashboardData, and derived types (ReadinessScore, CountdownResult, ThemeMode).
nodes.languageNotes: Single source of truth for all application types â€” all component props and lib functions reference these interfaces via the @/types import.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README for a high-level picture of the Camping Dashboard — an Algonquin wilderness mission control system built with Next.js, Supabase, and Leaflet.

tour.title: Type System — The Contract
tour.description: The types/index.ts file is the single source of truth for all TypeScript interfaces. Every component, helper, and lib function references these types. Understanding this file unlocks the entire data model.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: page.tsx is the dashboard orchestrator. It fetches all Supabase data, manages state for every user-authored entity, and renders the full card grid. The DashboardLoader/DashboardContent split handles async loading gracefully.

tour.title: Data Layer — Supabase Clients
tour.description: Two Supabase clients power the app: a browser-safe anon client for read/write from components, and a server-side admin client for privileged API route operations like weather sync.

tour.title: Business Logic — Readiness Engine
tour.description: helpers.ts contains all pure calculation functions. The readiness engine computes a weighted score across Gear (35%), Offline (20%), Meals (20%), Weather (15%), and Timeline (10%). This is the brain of the dashboard.

tour.title: Auth — Gate for Edit Controls
tour.description: authContext.tsx provides the isAuthorized flag that gates all CRUD mutations. When not authorized, card components receive undefined handlers and render in read-only mode.

tour.title: Weather Pipeline
tour.description: The refresh-weather API route fetches Open-Meteo data, transforms it via weatherMapper.ts, and upserts to Supabase. The Vercel cron in vercel.json calls this on a schedule.

tour.title: Feature Cards — The Dashboard Grid
tour.description: Each card is a self-contained display unit receiving data and optional mutation handlers as props. The Hero header, Map, Weather, Gear, Timeline, Meals, Crew, Alerts, and Readiness cards form the 12-column mission control layout.

tour.title: UI Primitives — The Design System
tour.description: The ui/ folder contains the reusable building blocks: GlassCard for panels, CrudSheet for slide-out forms, ProgressBar for metrics, and TopoBackground for the wilderness aesthetic.

project.name: Camping Dashboard
project.description: Algonquin Wilderness Mission Control — An expedition planning dashboard for a backcountry canoe trip to Maple Lake Site 4, featuring real-time weather, gear tracking, readiness scoring, and interactive mapping.
